_comment: Citation registry. Each species and management guide references these by id. Add your own sources here (e.g. your CIG trial reports) and reference them from local-observations.json.

nrcs-appalachia.title: Best Management of Pasture and Hayland Species in Appalachia
nrcs-appalachia.org: USDA NRCS Appalachian Plant Materials Center
nrcs-appalachia.note: Regional species adaptation and management guidance for the Appalachian region.

wvu-species.title: Forage Species
wvu-species.note: Species-by-species adaptation notes for West Virginia.

wvu-production.title: Pasture and Hay Production Potential in West Virginia
wvu-production.note: Yield and adaptation context for WV forages.

wvu-grazing.title: Grazing for Appalachian Sustainability
wvu-grazing.note: Rotational grazing and pasture management for Appalachian producers.

wvu-fertility.title: Soil Fertility & Fertilizers
wvu-fertility.note: Soil testing, liming, and fertility management; links to the WVU Fertility Recommend Tool and Forage Fertilization Worksheet (downloadable spreadsheets).

wvu-lime-tool.title: Value of Ag Limestone Tool (calculator)
wvu-lime-tool.note: Interactive calculator: compares lime products by Effective Neutralizing Value (ENV) so you can price them on an apples-to-apples basis and find the tons/acre needed.

wvu-blend-calc.title: Blended Fertilizer Calculator
wvu-blend-calc.org: West Virginia University Extension / Soil Testing Lab
wvu-blend-calc.note: Interactive calculator: enter your soil test's N-P-K recommendation and it works out how to blend basic fertilizers (urea, DAP, potash, etc.) to hit it.

wvu-soil-report.title: Using the WVU Soil Testing Report
wvu-soil-report.note: How to read a soil test report and translate it into a lime and fertilizer plan.

wvu-pelleted-lime.title: Low-rate Application of Pelleted Lime
wvu-pelleted-lime.note: Using pelleted (fines) lime at low rates via a fertilizer spreader when bulk ag lime spreading isn't practical — common on steep or small Appalachian fields.

psu-forage-maintenance.title: Soil Fertility Management for Forage Crops: Maintenance
psu-forage-maintenance.note: Maintaining P and K by replacing what forage removes; interpreting optimum/high soil-test categories.

vt-steep-pastures.title: Managing Virginia's Steep Pastures (VCE 418-005)
vt-steep-pastures.note: Species and grazing management for sloping Appalachian pastures.

psu-establishment.title: Successful Forage Crop Establishment
psu-establishment.note: Seeding depth, timing, seedbed, and no-till/frost seeding guidance.

psu-renovation.title: Hay and Pasture Renovation Decisions
psu-renovation.note: When and how to renovate versus reseed pastures.

psu-tall-fescue.title: Tall Fescue
psu-tall-fescue.note: Adaptation, endophyte, and management of tall fescue.

osu-fescue-ph.title: Tall Fescue Monograph: pH Suitability
osu-fescue-ph.org: Oregon State University Forage Information System
osu-fescue-ph.note: Detailed pH tolerance data for tall fescue.

msu-seeding-rates.title: Forage Seeding Rates When Seeded Alone or in Mixtures
msu-seeding-rates.note: Seeding-rate reference for monocultures and mixes.

mu-birdsfoot.title: Birdsfoot Trefoil (G4640)
mu-birdsfoot.note: Establishment and management of birdsfoot trefoil.

nrcs-plants.title: PLANTS Database & Plant Guides
nrcs-plants.note: Species fact sheets and plant guides, including native warm-season grasses.

forage-upick.title: Forage U-Pick
forage-upick.note: Precedent tool cited in the CIG grant that inspired this planner.

ssurgo.title: Web Soil Survey / SSURGO (Soil Data Access)
ssurgo.note: Authoritative soil map-unit data (pH, drainage class, slope, available water) queried by location for the address lookup.

usgs-3dep.title: 3DEP Elevation Point Query Service
usgs-3dep.note: Elevation, slope, and aspect for the address lookup.

open-meteo.title: Historical Weather / Climate API (ERA5, 1991–2020 normals)
open-meteo.note: Downscaled climate normals used to derive hardiness zone, frost-free days, and annual rainfall for the address lookup.

nominatim.title: Nominatim Geocoding
nominatim.note: Converts a typed address to coordinates in the browser. The address is used once and never stored.

ifas-calibration.title: Calibrating Forage Seeding Equipment (AG158/SS-AGR-90)
ifas-calibration.note: Bag/catch calibration procedure for drills and catch-pan procedure for broadcast spreaders, specific to forage seeding.

psu-spreader-calib.title: Calibrating Your Fertilizer Spreader
psu-spreader-calib.note: Two-direction (crosshatch) application at right angles, splitting the rate in half per pass, and spreader calibration by catch pan.

umd-seeding.title: Guidelines for Seeding New Pastures and Renovating Old Pastures (Part III: Seeding and Establishment)
umd-seeding.org: Maryland Department of Agriculture / University of Maryland Extension
umd-seeding.note: Seeding depth vs. soil texture, press wheels, first-year clipping and weed control, tug test, and first-grazing protocol.

ut-planting.title: Forage: Planting
ut-planting.org: University of Tennessee Beef & Forage Center
ut-planting.note: Forage planting dates, seedbed preparation, and establishment guidance.

msu-pasture-est.title: Guidelines for Pasture Establishment
msu-pasture-est.note: Firm-seedbed standard (footprint no deeper than ¼ inch), cultipacking, and no-till considerations.

uky-rest.title: The Importance of Rest Periods Between Grazing
uky-rest.note: Why rest length varies with species and season, and how paddock count sets the rest period.

umd-summer-grazing.title: Summer Grazing Management
umd-summer-grazing.note: Regrowth-based rest periods (graze again at 8–10 inches) and longer recovery in summer.

pb1752.title: Native Warm-Season Grasses: Identification, Establishment and Management (PB1752)
pb1752.org: University of Tennessee Extension (Harper, Bates, et al.)
pb1752.note: Authoritative manual on native warm-season grasses/forbs: PLS, seeding rates, depth, timing, and seed sources.

ernst-src.title: Ernst Conservation Seeds — species pages
ernst-src.note: Regional (Appalachian/Mid-Atlantic) grower of native and forage seed; species adaptation and availability.

roundstone-src.title: Roundstone Native Seed — species pages
roundstone-src.note: Regional native seed grower; species adaptation, seeding, and availability.

uky-kura.title: Kura Clover for Kentucky (AGR-141)
uky-kura.note: Establishment, pH, seeding rate, and management of kura clover.

usda-strawberry.title: Strawberry Clover Plant Guide
usda-strawberry.note: Adaptation and management of strawberry clover on wet/saline soils.

climatena.title: ClimateNA
climatena.org: Wang, Hamann, Spittlehouse & Carroll (Univ. of British Columbia)
climatena.note: Downscaled climate reference named in the CIG grant. Desktop/batch tool with no browser API; Open-Meteo normals are used as the live substitute. ClimateNA point exports can be added later.
